# feat(ml): add TrainingResult dataclass for kailash-ml 2.0

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `11b163333781` — feat(ml): add TrainingResult dataclass for kailash-ml 2.0

**Body**:

Implements specs/ml-engines.md §4 as the single envelope every
training path produces.

Required fields (§4.1): model_uri, metrics, device_used, accelerator,
precision, elapsed_seconds, tracker_run_id, tenant_id, artifact_uris,
lightning_trainer_config.

Optional fields (§4.1): family, hyperparameters, split_info,
calibration, feature_importance.

__post_init__ validates (§4.2 MUST 1): all required fields populated
(tracker_run_id and tenant_id legitimately nullable); metrics are
finite floats (NaN/Inf blocked per ml-tracking.md §2.5 - non-finite
metrics poison downstream aggregation); device_used / accelerator /
precision are concrete non-empty strings and never "auto" (§4.2 MUST 2
lightning_trainer_config carries what ACTUALLY ran); elapsed_seconds
is finite and non-negative.

to_dict / from_dict follow the EATP wire contract (ml-engines.md
§10.1) so a Python-trained model registered from kailash-py is
loadable by a kailash-rs registry reader. Unknown keys in from_dict
are rejected rather than silently dropped so wire-format drift
surfaces at read time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
